name: Mynd Property Management API
what_it_does: Mynd REST API for technology-enabled single-family rental (SFR) property management platform targeting institutional investors. Enables AI agents to manage property onboarding and inspection workflow data for SFR portfolio management, handle tenant acquisition, leasing, and rent collection workflow automation for rental operations, access maintenance request routing and vendor work order management for property maintenance, retrieve property performance analytics and NOI data for investor portfolio reporting, manage vacancy tracking and make-ready process workflow for rental turnover optimization, handle eviction workflow data and legal compliance tracking for tenant management, access market rent analysis and pricing recommendation data for revenue optimization, retrieve regulatory compliance data for rent control and habitability requirements, manage tenant communication and satisfaction data for tenant retention analytics, and integrate property data with institutional investor reporting and asset management platforms.
use_cases: Automating rental property onboarding from SFR portfolio agents
Managing maintenance workflows from property operations agents
Tracking tenant rent payments from financial reporting agents
Optimizing rental pricing from revenue management agents
not_for: Commercial real estate without SFR residential management focus
DIY landlords without institutional scale portfolio management
Build-to-rent development without operational property management
best_when: An institutional SFR investor or property owner using Mynd wants AI agents to automate property onboarding, tenant management, maintenance workflows, vacancy optimization, and portfolio performance reporting across a distributed SFR portfolio.
avoid_when: LEGAL RISK: Mynd manages eviction and tenant legal proceedings — automated eviction workflow without attorney review violates tenant protection laws (CARES Act, just cause eviction ordinances). Rent collection automation must comply with FDCPA for delinquent accounts. Automated rent increases must validate local rent control ordinances (California AB 1482, NYC RSA). Habitability automation without licensed contractor work creates landlord liability.

auth.notes: Mynd uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Institutional client credentials. No webhooks documented. REST API with JSON. Oakland, California HQ. Founded 2016. VC-backed ($180M+ raised, Lightspeed, Canaan, others). Technology-first single-family rental management platform. 20,000+ homes managed. Institutional SFR focus (not DIY landlords). Competes with Roofstock, Progress Residential, and traditional PM firms for institutional SFR management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — institutional client relationship required
pricing.paid_tiers: Management fee percentage of rent; API access for institutional partners
pricing.notes: Oakland, California. Founded 2016. VC-backed: Lightspeed Venture Partners, Canaan Partners, QED Investors ($180M+). 20,000+ managed homes across US markets. Institutional SFR investors as primary clients (pension funds, REITs, family offices). Technology platform includes inspection apps, pricing algorithms, and investor portals. Competes with Progress Residential and FirstKey Homes for SFR management market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; client-negotiated

known_agent_gotchas: LEGAL RISK: Eviction workflow automation requires attorney review in each state; automated eviction initiation without legal counsel violates tenant due process and state-specific just cause requirements
Rent control compliance — California AB 1482 and local ordinances cap annual rent increases; automated rent pricing must validate rent control applicability before increase

FDCPA for delinquent accounts — debt collection communications for delinquent rent must comply with FDCPA; automated collection messages without FDCPA-compliant language creates consumer protection violations
SFR-specific API maturity — Mynd API is primarily for institutional partners, not public developers; API documentation and stability may be less mature than enterprise SaaS vendors
Habitability standards — automated maintenance closure without verifying habitability standards are met creates implied warranty of habitability landlord liability
Security deposit handling — automated security deposit deduction without tenant documentation and state-compliant accounting creates security deposit dispute liability
agent_readiness.security_notes: SFR property management. SOC2. OAuth2/API key. US. Tenant, property, and financial rental data.

security_score_components.security_notes: SFR property management. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Tenant and property data.
